cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is about what we know about garage sales based on the provided passage. The options are A through D. First, I need to recall the details from the text. The passage mentions that garage sales are held usually in the spring and fall when the weather is good. So that's when they happen. Then it says these have become part of American culture. The text also talks about homeowners clearing out items they no longer find useful and advertising in local papers. The goods are in good condition, and you can find great buys if you search carefully. It also says that if the owner is selling their home or moving, they might sell items far below their true worth. Looking at the options: A) It is only held in the spring of the year. But the text says "usually in the spring and fall," so "only" is incorrect. So A is wrong. B) The goods on display are totally useless. The passage says the goods are in good condition and are items the homeowner no longer needs. So "totally useless" is not accurate. B is incorrect. C) Garage sales are popular with Americans. The passage states that weekend outdoor garage sales have become very much a part of American culture. That implies they are popular.
